content.problemStatement:
Janmansh is at the fruit market to buy fruits for Chingari. There is an infinite supply of three different kinds of fruits with prices AAA, BBB and CCC.

He needs to buy a total of XXX fruits having at least 222 different kinds of fruits. What is the least amount of money he can spend to buy fruits?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space separated integers XXX, AAA, BBB, CCC - the number of fruits to buy and the prices of the three different types of fruits respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the least amount of money he can spend to buy fruits.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers X A B C — the total number of fruits to buy and the prices of the three fruit types.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum total amount of money Janmansh has to spend.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: cheapest price = 1, second cheapest = 1, answer = (2‑1)*1 + 1 = 2.\nTest case 2: sorted prices = [2,3,4], answer = (3‑1)*2 + 3 = 7.

solution.approach: For each test case sort the three prices in non‑decreasing order: p1 <= p2 <= p3. To satisfy the requirement of using at least two different fruit kinds, the cheapest possible purchase is to take (X‑1) fruits of the cheapest price p1 and exactly one fruit of the second cheapest price p2. Any other combination would replace a fruit of price p1 with a fruit of price >= p2, increasing the total cost. Hence the answer is (X‑1)*p1 + p2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – sorting three numbers is O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it)); A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it)); C = int(next(it))
        p = sorted([A, B, C])
        ans = (X - 1) * p[0] + p[1]
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
